summary: COMPLETENESS VERDICT: _ephinea_hooks.txt (120 lines / 119 unique VAs) is a PARTIAL capture of ONE installer family (5-byte E9 JMP trampolines), NOT the full detour set. I independently enumerated the Ephinea detour installer in the unpacked dll with radare2: the installer is a flat sequence of `push <psobbVA>; push <handler 0x78exxxxx>; call <install>` records (e.g. at file 0x7b42df79: `6a05 68 a15a4100 68 00c2e578 e8...` = push len5, push VA 0x415aa1, push handler 0x78e5c200). Strict two-push records = 215 unique target VAs; hooks.txt contains only 99 of them => hooks.txt MISSES ~116 installer detours (~45-55% miss rate). SEPARATELY, the delta CSV shows 287 opcode-aligned E9-JMP sites + 107 E8 (in-place CALL) detour sites = 394 patched instruction sites, plus ~2000 eph_only data/scalar writes (2451 total changed VAs). hooks.txt entirely omits the E8 CALL-detour family (55x +0x334 16-bit-store HUD-quad scalers + 7x +0x2bc). GOOD NEWS: the 956-record digest is FAR more complete than hooks.txt because it was mined from the delta CSV — of the 116 installer detours hooks.txt missed, 114 are present in the delta (recoverable by the digest) and the other 2 (0x007318DD, 0x0080616E) are second JMPs absorbed into adjacent multi-byte delta rows, so effectively 0 are truly invisible. The digest's main DEFECTS are classification, not coverage: (1) it labels hundreds of eph_only widescreen_anchor .data rows 'match high' when our client wrote NOTHING there (441 eph_only vs 299 differ in 0x008F-0x0092 region) — 'match' is being over-claimed on unverified static writes our client never replicated; (2) the 0x009A3840 'particle_title' table is actually Trinity's valScaleI START-SCREEN BG TILES (title atlas), not photon-blast particles; (3) site 0x5474d4 is double-counted as both 'deanchor' and 'hud_quad' (one detour, handler=FUN_52db2240=deanchor). KEY SUBSYSTEM ANSWERS: char-create dressing-room tiles — Ephinea does NOT statically touch ANY Trinity dressingRoomTilesFloat (0x0091DBxx), dressingRoomTiles W/H (0x009B77xx); it only tweaks the gradient COLORS (0x009B8DB7/BB/BF/C3 single-byte alpha edits = Trinity dressingRoomRightGradientColors region) — so Ephinea relies on runtime per-draw detours for tile layout, genuinely lacking static tile scaling Trinity has. window_mode/borderless IS present (0x0082C774 = 36-byte NOP of the stock CreateWindowEx windowed path; 0x0082D1E9, 0x007C16BE retarget device/window globals) and is correctly flagged 'missing' from our coverage. D3D Device::Reset re-apply: NO Reset hook found in handlers (grep only hit CRT noise) — confirms PATCHES_OVERVIEW 'plausible-but-unconfirmed'; cascade re-runs on config reload only. particle_title: Ephinea RESCALES the title effect (0x0078515F/516A E9 detours + value rescales like 100.0f->scaled at 0x00785679/6FB/C0A), it does NOT suppress it — so the 'leak to char-select' is almost certainly OUR scene-teardown artifact, not a missing Ephinea suppression hook.
missingSubsystems: E8 CALL-detour family ENTIRELY ABSENT from hooks.txt: 55x in-place '+0x334' 16-bit-store HUD-quad anchor scalers (stock '66 89 xx 34 03 00 00' = mov [reg+0x334],ax -> CALL FUN_52dc4f60-class scaler) + 7x '+0x2bc' stores. This is a structurally distinct installer family (CALL replacement, not JMP trampoline) that hooks.txt's E9-only capture cannot see.

~116 of 215 installer detour-target VAs are absent from hooks.txt (radare2-enumerated). Examples of missed installer VAs: 0x0043E9BB, 0x0043EBEF, 0x0045B93A, 0x004D429E/42C7/42EB, 0x0050DB04, 0x005284EA, 0x00542B1A, 0x005C36CC, 0x005CAA9D. 114/116 are recoverable from the delta CSV (so the 956-digest likely has them).

window_mode / borderless virtual-fullscreen: PRESENT in Ephinea but under-covered by us. 0x0082C774 (36-byte NOP of stock windowed CreateWindowEx path: stock pushes 0x1E0=480/0x280=640 + ff15 indirect call), 0x0082D1E9 (24-byte device-global retarget on [0xACBED8]), 0x007C16BE (27-byte window-init retarget), 0x0098A323/326 (.data aspect-ratio float). Digest flags these 'window_mode missing' correctly.

charcreate_tiles tile POSITION+SIZE: Ephinea genuinely has NO static patch for Trinity's dressingRoomTilesFloat (0x0091DBD0-0x0091DC0C) or dressingRoomTiles W/H (0x009B77D0-0x009B7836) — confirmed all NOT in delta. Trinity statically scales these by hudScaleY; Ephinea does not, so char-create tile layout in Ephinea is handled (if at all) only by the runtime per-draw detour cascade. This is a real architectural divergence the digest should call out, not a missed hook.

D3D IDirect3DDevice9::Reset re-apply hook: NOT FOUND in Ephinea handlers (no Reset/PresentParam/DeviceLost/SetCooperativeLevel reference in decompiled handlers; only CRT format-string noise). Cascade re-runs proven only on config reload via tail-call chain. Reset re-apply remains unconfirmed/absent — consistent with PATCHES_OVERVIEW.
suspectClassifications: 0x009A3840/3848/3854/385C table labeled 'particle_title' is MISLABELED — Trinity resolution.h valScaleI (L82-115) names these exact VAs 'Start Screen Tile 7/6/5/4 X/Y/W/H' = the TITLE-SCREEN background tile atlas, not the photon-blast particle effect. Also note EphLive=ac vs IoLive=aa here (our client AND Ephinea both edit this title table to DIFFERENT values = the wordmark-doubling area).

Mass 'widescreen_anchor match high' over-claim: hundreds of 0x008Fxxxx-0x0092xxxx rows the digest calls 'match' are eph_only in the delta (IoLive BLANK = our client wrote NOTHING). Region split = 441 eph_only vs 299 differ. 'match' should mean IoLive present AND == EphLive; applying 'match high' to eph_only rows is a category error that masks a large coverage gap (or at best reflects an unverified assumption that our runtime rebake covers them).

0x5474d4 is DOUBLE-COUNTED: digest has '0x5474d4 deanchor perframe_rebake high' AND '0x005474D5 hud_quad differ high' for the same single detour (0x005474D5 is just the JMP-displacement byte of the 0x5474d4 site). Handler is FUN_52db2240 = deanchor pin, so the 'hud_quad' label is wrong; it is one deanchor detour.

The 115 '565555' (Eph) vs '545555' (Io) near-match pairs labeled 'match high' differ in the float LSB (1-ulp mantissa). Defensible as match but technically byte-different; confidence should acknowledge sub-ulp divergence rather than asserting exact 'high' match.

audio-cluster VAs (0x005DDD47, 0x00642C3B, 0x005E4F9C, etc. 'audio na') and most 'other na' rows in 0x005Exxxx-0x0066xxxx are NOT in hooks.txt — their subsystem is INFERRED from address neighborhood (delta-only), not handler-confirmed. Confidence 'med/high' on these is optimistic; they are unverified guesses.
unaccountedConcerns: hooks.txt is missing the E8 in-place CALL detour family wholesale (62 records) — any analysis that treats hooks.txt as the detour ground truth will systematically miss the HUD-quad anchor scaler subsystem (FUN_52dc4f60 applied as CALL replacements at +0x334/+0x2bc stores).

The ~28-record gap between the loose handler-push count (244) and strict two-push count (216) implies a THIRD install-record encoding (e.g. computed VA, push-len 0x0a variant, or single-push helper) not yet enumerated — there may be detours that neither hooks.txt nor the strict r2 scan caught. 0x78e7xxxx handler band (127 records) is the largest and least sampled.

473 rows where EphLive != IoLive with IoLive present = ACTIVE divergences between our client and Ephinea (271 in 0x009xxxxx, 82 in 0x006, 49 in 0x007). These are where our build measurably differs from Ephinea's widescreen output and are the highest-value targets; the digest's coarse 'differ' label does not rank them by visual impact.

0x008F8328 / 0x008F8060/8068/841C show STRUCTURAL (not 1-ulp) divergence between EphLive and IoLive (inserted/different tokens) in the HUDWidth/anchor pointer-table region — these are concrete unreconciled differences, likely related to the known psobb.io NN-NN bar / HUDWidth 0x008f8494 issues in MEMORY.

The 0x006Cxxxx 'differ high' blocks (44 rows a4ba00->94d405, 29 rows 04->20) are classified 'other' but both our client and Ephinea rewrite them identically-in-kind — likely a shared runtime/library region (CRT or engine init), not widescreen; left unclassified, could hide a non-widescreen behavioral patch.
recommendations: DEFINITIVE installer enumeration: in the unpacked dll, disassemble the installer span (file ~0x7b41ae00-0x7b44d460, the push/push/call sequence) and extract EVERY (VA,handler,len) triple. r2 one-liner that worked: `r2 -q -e bin.relocs.apply=false -c '/x 6800000000680000e478e8:ff00000000ff0000ffffff' <dll>` swept over handler high-bytes e4/e5/e6/e7 gave 216 strict records (215 unique VAs). Re-run for the 0x6a-push-len variant and any single-push form to close the 216-vs-244 gap, then diff the full VA list against the 956 digest to find true gaps.

Re-derive 'match' status strictly from the delta CSV Class column: only label a VA 'match' when IoLive is NON-EMPTY and == EphLive. Re-label all eph_only rows currently marked 'match high' as 'missing' (our client absent) or 'covered-by-runtime-rebake (unverified)'. This will correct the 441-row over-claim in the 0x008F-0x0092 anchor region.

Relabel the 0x009A3840 table from 'particle_title' to 'particle_title/title_bg_tiles' per Trinity valScaleI; separate the genuine photon-effect detours (0x0078515F/516A E9 + 0x00785679/6FB/C0A rescales) into their own bucket. Then test the char-select photon leak in OUR build with the title-effect teardown forced — if Ephinea merely rescales (proven here), the leak is our scene-transition bug, fixable on our side without a new hook.

For char-create tiles: since Ephinea does NOT statically patch dressingRoomTilesFloat/Tiles (confirmed not-in-delta), set a live breakpoint on Ephinea's per-draw detours (FUN_52da6e50 aspect @0x67c4ed, FUN_52dc4f60 hud_quad @0x78a300, FUN_52db2240 deanchor) while in the dressing room to see whether Ephinea scales these tiles at RUNTIME or leaves them 4:3. Compare to Trinity's static hudScaleY approach to decide our fix (the 'still fucked' tiles).

De-duplicate 0x5474d4/0x005474D5 (one deanchor detour) and audit all digest rows where a site and site+1 both appear with different subsystems — these are JMP-opcode + displacement halves of a single detour being counted twice across subsystems.

Verify the inferred 'audio'/'coop_net'/'other na' clusters (0x005Dxxxx-0x0066xxxx, 0x00B1xxxx-0x00B4xxxx) by resolving their handler via the installer triple (now extractable) and naming the hooked VA against original-psobb-client-source — replace neighborhood-inference classifications with handler-confirmed ones before trusting the subsystem taxonomy counts.
